info.title: RUN Apparel API
info.description: Comprehensive API documentation for the RUN-Remix platform, including Media Asset Management, Product Catalog, and Contact Services.

tags.name: Upload
tags.description: Single, batch, and chunked upload operations

tags.name: Folder Management
tags.description: Organize media assets into hierarchical folders

tags.name: Admin Operations
tags.description: Administrative endpoints for media maintenance and validation (requires admin authentication)

get.description: Retrieve paginated list of products. See /api/products/by-path for details.
Retrieves the dynamic configuration for the contact page, including Hero title, contact details, working hours, and social links.
Retrieves a list of business office locations including coordinates.

post.description: Submits a contact inquiry via the contact page form.

get.description: Retrieves paginated list of media assets with optional filtering by type, folder, and active status. Results are cached for performance.
Returns total count of active media assets. Result is cached.
Search media assets by filename or original name using database-level ILIKE filtering. Supports pagination.
Retrieves a single media asset by ID. Result is cached.

patch.description: Updates media asset metadata. Invalidates relevant caches.

delete.description: Soft deletes media asset from database and removes files from Object Storage. Uses compensating transaction pattern for atomicity.

post.description: Uploads a single file with magic number validation, automatic thumbnail generation, and metadata extraction. On successful upload, automatically invalidates all media list caches to ensure the new asset appears immediately in queries without manual refresh.
Uploads a file from Base64 encoded string. Useful for browser-based applications.

data.description: Base64 encoded file data (with or without data URI prefix)

post.description: Uploads a complete GLTF/GLB package with associated textures and resources. Automatically processes and links related files.
Initializes a multi-part chunked upload session for large files. Returns uploadId to use in subsequent chunk uploads.
Uploads a single chunk of a multi-part upload. Chunks are validated and stored temporarily until finalization.
Uploads a chunk as raw binary data (alternative to multipart). Useful for custom upload implementations.
Finalizes a chunked upload by combining all chunks, validating integrity, and creating the media asset record.

get.description: Retrieves the current progress of a chunked upload session.

delete.description: Cancels an in-progress chunked upload and cleans up temporary files.

get.description: Lists all active (in-progress) chunked upload sessions.

post.description: Performs batch upload of multiple files or batch delete of media assets. Uses compensating transactions for atomicity. Supports both multipart file upload and JSON delete requests. On successful operations, automatically invalidates all media list caches to ensure changes appear immediately.

ids.description: Array of media asset IDs to delete

get.description: Retrieves content for multiple media assets in a single request. Returns a JSON object mapping IDs to content URLs.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of media asset IDs

get.description: Retrieves the actual file content for a media asset. Returns the raw file with appropriate content-type header.
Retrieves the thumbnail image for a media asset. Automatically generated for images and 3D models.
Retrieves geometry metadata for 3D models (GLTF/GLB). Includes polygon count, bounding box, and material information.
Proxies media content through the API server. Useful for CORS-restricted environments or when direct Object Storage access is not available.
Proxies thumbnail content through the API server.
Retrieves media usage analytics including upload trends, storage usage, and access patterns.
Comprehensive performance metrics dashboard including API response times, cache hit rates, and system health.
Detailed metrics about upload operations including success rates, average sizes, and failure reasons.
Real-time performance metrics for API endpoints.
Overall system health status including database connectivity, Object Storage status, and cache availability.
Comprehensive health check scanning all system components and dependencies.
Retrieves comprehensive media cache statistics including hit rates, memory usage, and key counts for media-specific caches.

Event-driven cache invalidation endpoint that returns the latest invalidation timestamp for a given pattern. Used by frontend polling to detect backend cache invalidations and trigger automatic UI updates. Part of the Phase 4 & 5 two-layer cache invalidation architecture (backend event bus + frontend polling).

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Cache pattern to check (e.g., 'media:' for all media-related caches)

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds of the latest invalidation event

400.description: Bad Request - Missing required pattern parameter

post.description: Clears all cache entries related to a specific media asset.

get.description: Tests connectivity to Replit Object Storage by attempting a read/write operation.

post.description: Scans and repairs database integrity issues including orphaned records, missing references, and constraint violations.
Scans media assets and repairs incorrect or missing MIME types based on file extensions and magic numbers.

get.description: Retrieves all folders. Optionally includes children for each folder.

parameters.name: includeChildren
parameters.description: Include child folders for each folder

post.description: Creates a new folder for organizing media assets.

parentId.description: Parent folder ID for nested folders

get.description: Retrieves hierarchical tree structure of all folders with nested children.
Retrieves a specific folder by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a folder. Fails if folder contains child folders.

get.description: Retrieves all direct child folders of a specified folder.
Retrieves all media assets within a specific folder.

post.description: Moves a media asset to a specified folder.

mediaAssetId.description: ID of media asset to move

post.description: Administrative endpoint to sanitize and fix URLs for all media assets. Validates each asset using MediaValidator and updates any invalid or malformed URLs. Requires admin authentication.

message.example: URL fix completed: 15 URLs fixed, 0 errors

post.description: Administrative endpoint to find and fix corrupted media URLs in category featured content. Specifically targets URLs containing 'undefined' or '/api/media/proxy/undefined' and clears them. Requires admin authentication.

message.example: Corrupted media cleanup completed: 3 categories fixed

post.description: Administrative endpoint to validate uploaded media files for security threats and malicious signatures. Accepts multipart file upload and checks each file for potential threats. Requires admin authentication.

files.description: Files to validate for security threats

children.description: Nested child folders (only included when requested)

NotFound.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

RateLimitError.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before retrying

example.error: Too many media requests, please try again later

PayloadTooLarge.description: Payload Too Large - File exceeds maximum size

UnsupportedMediaType.description: Unsupported Media Type - File type not allowed

example.error: File type not supported or magic number validation failed
